Model Picks

A transparent lean for every game — team quality, recent form, and the pitching matchup, with the math shown on every card.

How it works: team strength blends Pythagorean win% (from run differential — 75%) with last-10 form (25%). The two strengths are combined head-to-head (log5) and the home team gets the historical ~54% home-field bump. Then the starting pitcher matchup shifts the number: each run of season-ERA advantage moves the favorite by roughly 4–5 points (starters with under 20 innings count as league average). With your Odds API key saved, the model is compared to the market's no-vig probability — Value only appears when the model beats the price by 3+ points.
Crunching the numbers…
These are model outputs, not guarantees. The model still doesn't know about injuries, bullpen fatigue, weather, or lineups — and season ERA is a noisy measure of pitcher skill. Treat picks as a starting point for your own analysis, never as a reason to bet more than you can afford to lose.
